title: Kaboom Crew pre-game platform â€” orthographic camera, damped follow, 2D HUD, grid primitives, generator framework

stories.title: Orthographic camera path in the render adapter
stories.summary: Today `ThreeRenderAdapter.acquireCamera` always constructs a `PerspectiveCamera`. Kaboom Crew's top-down arcade view + the existing `shadows-bench` RTS camera both want orthographic projection (cleaner gameplay readability, no foreshortening artefacts on the grid). Extend the scene `Camera` component schema with a `projection: "perspective" | "orthographic"` field (default perspective for backward compat); ortho mode reads `orthographicSize` + `near` / `far` from the same component. WebGPU + WebGL paths both honour the projection field. `engine inspect` reports it. Existing scenes keep perspective by absence; opting a scene into ortho is a one-line edit.
verification: schemas/components/camera.schema.json gains projection + orthographicSize fields
examples/hello-3d with `projection: "orthographic"` still passes engine:check + renders without TypeScript / runtime errors on WebGL and WebGPU
live playwright probe at hello-3d shows an orthographic view (no foreshortening) when the field is flipped
schemas/components/camera.schema.json gained orthographicSize; allOf-if requires it when kind=orthographic
engine:check on hello-3d with orthographic + size â€” OK; without size â€” AGF_SCHEMA_REQUIRED_PROPERTY
typecheck passes; 517 unit tests passing
resize handler branches on PerspectiveCamera vs OrthographicCamera; CSM rebuild skips with a warn when active camera is orthographic

stories.title: Damped follow camera system + `FollowTarget` / `CameraDamping` components
stories.summary: Engine-level `DampedFollowCameraSystem` reads a `FollowTarget { entityId, offset, lookAtOffset }` and a `CameraDamping { positionTau, rotationTau, lookAheadMs }` component on a camera entity. Outputs a smoothed camera transform per frame (exponential damping driven by frameDeltaSeconds â€” works under variable frame time, unlike fixed-tau). Look-ahead extrapolates the target's recent velocity by `lookAheadMs`; Kaboom Crew uses ~120 ms. Lives under `engine/render/systems/damped-follow-camera-system.ts`. Bench-camera follow already exists for shadows-bench (RtsCameraSystem) â€” that one stays sample-local; this is the generic primitive every game can pick up.
verification: schemas/components/follow-target.schema.json + damped-camera.schema.json validate
hello-3d gains an opt-in playtest scene that follows a moving entity smoothly with no jitter
system is registered through scheduler, no DOM access, no Three.js import outside engine/render

stories.title: 2D HUD runtime primitive (`engine/runtime/ui/`)
stories.summary: Engine ships an HTML-overlay HUD primitive so games don't keep rolling ad-hoc DOM (beacon-world's HP/SIG widget pattern). The runtime mounts a single `<div data-agf-hud>` adjacent to the canvas, exposes `runtime.hud.add({ id, render, slot })` / `runtime.hud.update(id, data)` / `runtime.hud.remove(id)`. `slot` is one of `topLeft | topRight | bottomLeft | bottomRight | center`. Per-widget render is a pure function `(data) => string | HTMLElement`. Strict CSP-friendly (no innerHTML on raw strings â€” the runtime escapes); fully removable when the runtime tears down. Lives under `engine/runtime/ui/hud.ts`; DOM dependency stays inside this single file.
verification: hello-3d playtest renders one HUD widget showing fps + entity count
removing the runtime disposes the HUD root and all widget DOM nodes
no other engine module imports `engine/runtime/ui/` (single-DOM-touchpoint property)

stories.title: Generic minimap widget on top of the HUD runtime
stories.summary: Project-agnostic minimap widget: a `<canvas>`-backed HUD slot that takes a list of `{ x, z, color, shape: dot | triangle | rect, size }` markers + a `bounds: { minX, maxX, minZ, maxZ }` and draws them at the configured slot. Project code queries the ECS each frame (cached query) and pushes the marker list via `runtime.hud.update`. Kaboom Crew's player / bots / objectives all share this one widget; no project-specific minimap implementation is needed. Lives under `engine/runtime/ui/minimap.ts` next to `hud.ts`.
verification: hello-3d playtest renders a minimap with one marker tracking the player
widget supports 200 markers without measurable jank (Canvas2D fillRect loop is the v0; WebGL or instanced path is a follow-up)

stories.title: `GridPosition` + `GridOccupant` components + `Grid` config schema
stories.summary: Engine grid primitives: `Grid { cellSize, originX, originZ, sizeX, sizeZ }` lives on a scene-level config entity (or scene.metadata). `GridPosition { gx, gz }` carries the cell coordinates; `GridOccupant { layer, blocksMovement, blocksBlast }` describes how the entity interacts with grid queries. Conversion helpers `worldToGrid(grid, worldVec3): { gx, gz }` and `gridToWorld(grid, gx, gz): Vec3` ship as pure functions under `engine/core/grid.ts`. Schema validation on out-of-bounds cell ids. Lives under `engine/core/` because it's reusable across roguelike / tower-defense / RTS / Kaboom Crew.
verification: schemas/components/grid-position.schema.json validates valid + invalid fixtures
engine:check rejects a scene where `GridPosition.gx` lies outside `Grid.sizeX`
unit tests cover worldToGrid / gridToWorld round-trip

stories.title: `GridOccupancySystem` â€” sparse cell â†’ entity index
stories.summary: ECS system that maintains a `Map<cellKey, EntityId[]>` derived from every entity with both `GridPosition` and `GridOccupant`. Invalidates on add / remove / move. Exposes `runtime.grid.occupants(gx, gz, layer?)` for project code (Kaboom Crew bomb placement, blast propagation, AI danger-map all hit this). Cache lifetime survives HMR; `world.query` style cached handle. Authoring rule: project gameplay never calls `world.query()` over GridPosition entities â€” always hit `runtime.grid.occupants`.
verification: unit test: moving an entity updates both old and new cell occupancy
incremental index matches a from-scratch rebuild (parity test similar to M16-cache-parity)

stories.title: `GridMover` component + `GridMovementSystem` (smooth tween between cells)
stories.summary: Component shape: `GridMover { speed, queuedDirection: { dx, dz }, currentLerp: 0..1 }`. System interpolates the entity's render Transform between the start cell (gx,gz) and the target cell over `1 / speed` seconds, then snaps to the new GridPosition and pulls the next queuedDirection. Plays well with `GridOccupancySystem` (movement only commits to the new cell when free + not blocked). Lane-assist: if the queuedDirection's target is blocked, the system tries the perpendicular cardinal first (so you don't get stuck on a corner). Lives under `engine/core/systems/grid-movement-system.ts`. Project code (Kaboom Crew player controller, bot AI) writes `queuedDirection` from input; system does the rest.
verification: playtest moves a Capsule across a 5Ă—5 grid hitting blocked cells; visits 4 distinct cells with no NaN transforms
unit test: blocked queued direction triggers lane-assist (perpendicular fallback)

stories.title: Seed-driven procedural generator framework + first Kaboom Crew level template
stories.summary: Generator framework lives under `engine/tools/generators/`. Surface: `generateScene({ seed, template, params }): SceneFile` where `template` is a registered generator function in a project's `bootstrap.ts`. Generators are pure functions (Mulberry32 PRNG) â†’ JSON scene. Engine ships: (a) the PRNG primitive (already in `engine/core/random.ts`?  check; add if missing), (b) a `validateGenerated(scene): Diagnostic[]` pass that runs the same `engine check` rules as static scenes, (c) one CLI: `engine generate <projectDir> --template <name> --seed <int> --out <file>`. First Kaboom Crew template: `kaboom-arena-small` â€” soft-blocks scattered around hard walls in a 15Ă—11 grid with 1 player spawn + 1 bot spawn. The template generator lives under `examples/kaboom-crew/generators/`.
verification: `engine generate examples/kaboom-crew --template kaboom-arena-small --seed 1` writes a JSON scene; `engine check` on it passes
same seed yields a byte-identical scene (deterministic)
different seeds yield different scenes (a quick diff prints â‰Ą10 differing entity ids)

stories.title: `examples/kaboom-crew/` project scaffold
stories.summary: Scaffold `examples/kaboom-crew/` as an AGF project: `project.json` (id `kaboom-crew`, agfFormatVersion 1, render.mode webgpu, performance budget), `bootstrap.ts` (registers the generator + a Kaboom-Crew-local set of placeholder components for upcoming bomb / block work), `scenes/start.scene.json` (small primitive-only test arena loaded by the runtime), `assets/_sources/asset-sources.json`, `prefabs/` skeleton (one player capsule, one bot capsule, one soft-block cube, one hard-block cube). README points to `notes/DynaBomber.md` for the codename history + Kaboom Crew memory entry. `engine:check examples/kaboom-crew` passes. No gameplay code yet â€” that lands as S082+ stories.
verification: npm run engine:check -- examples/kaboom-crew exits 0
npm run engine:summarize -- examples/kaboom-crew lists â‰Ą4 declared prefabs
npm run dev with the project selector lands on a primitive Kaboom Crew start scene with the player capsule + 3-4 soft / hard blocks visible

followUps: S082: bot AI primitives (danger map + basic pathfinding), bomb / blast / pickup pipeline as project-local Kaboom Crew systems, win/loss state.
Procedural character generator (the standalone tool) stays parked until MVP 0 ships; capsule placeholders are fine for now.
Audio system stays out of MVP 0 â€” sound is part of MVP 2.
outOfScope: Bombs, blasts, pickups, power-ups â€” all S082 work.
Multiplayer, client prediction, snapshot relevance â€” KABOOM-CREW-MVP-1 epic.
Procedural character generator â€” own epic, post-MVP-0.
Decal-on-grid renderer / region rules (M27 / M28) â€” required for MVP 2, not MVP 0.
notes: First sprint with the new epic graph in place â€” every story tags KABOOM-CREW-MVP-0 so the doctor + the HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG view rolls them up automatically.
Order-of-operations rationale lives in notes/dynabomber-readiness-analysis.md Â§11.
